operational_knowledge_units.title: SortLocationsDatabaseModel stores sorter locations and associated queue locations
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide states that the SortLocationsDatabaseModel table contains all sorter locations and their respective queue locations. Queue locations are defined as the locations behind the sorter location where an AGV with an empty tote moves to fill during an exchange.
observed_evidence: “The SortLocationsDatabaseModel table contains all the sorter locations and their respective queue locations.”
“The queue locations are the locations behind the sorter location where the agv with an empty tote will move to fill when an exchange happens.”

relationships.to: AGV with empty tote moves to fill during exchange

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: SortLocationsDatabaseModel database table reference. Contains all sorter locations and their corresponding queue locations. Queue locations are behind the sorter location and are used when an AGV carrying an empty tote moves forward to fill during an exchange.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Queue locations are ordered from the sorter entrance queue and tied to sorter side
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide indicates that the database model also records which sorter side each location is associated with and the sequence of those locations from the sorter entrance queue.
observed_evidence: “This table also includes which sorterside those locations are associated with as well as their sequence from the sorter entrance queue.”
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: SortLocationsDatabaseModel also stores sorterside association and the sequence ordering of locations from the sorter entrance queue. Useful for understanding queue position and side mapping for sorter-related locations.

operational_knowledge_units.title: During exchange, an AGV with an empty tote advances from queue location to fill sorter position
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide describes an operational behavior tied to the queue model: when an exchange occurs, an AGV with an empty tote moves from the queue location behind the sorter location to fill that position.
observed_evidence: “The queue locations are the locations behind the sorter location where the agv with an empty tote will move to fill when an exchange happens.”
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Operational behavior for sorter exchange: queue locations sit behind sorter locations, and when an exchange happens an AGV with an empty tote moves forward from that queue position to fill the sorter location.
